title: Crytal Nest
content:

{{Quote|<nowiki />''[…]'' we are currently negotiating with the Shashay regarding the establishment of a base on their homeworld. Due to the extreme security measures taken by the Shashay to conceal the coordinates of their world, we believe that this base would be totally secure.|Commander Zeke Rondel, on the Rebel Alliance|<ref name="Abduction" />}}
'''Crytal Nest''' was a [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Shashay]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. During the existence of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Shashay [[Scout/Legends|explored]] and [[Merchant/Legends|traded]] with other [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], but later into the Empire's [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]], the species became more reclusive. They kept the location of Crytal Nest a closely guarded secret, and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] eventually attempted to negotiate a treaty with the Shashay that would allow for the establishment of a base there.

Matters were complicated when [[Chief Administrator/Legends|Chief Administrator]] [[Braig Farool]] of [[TransGalMeg Industries, Incorporated]] and his brother [[Nak Farool|Nak]], [[Special Assistant]] to the [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector's]] Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Nile Owen]], had the famous Shashay [[Musician/Legends|performer]] [[Crying Dawn Singer]] [[Operation 45RA|kidnapped]] in order to discredit the Alliance and ensure the alignment of the Shashay to the Empire instead. Eventually, however, a team of Rebel agents rescued Crying Dawn Singer, and the Shashay and the Alliance ratified their treaty on Crytal Nest.

==Description==
Crytal Nest was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Abduction" /> located in the [[Crytal Nest system/Legends|Crytal Nest system]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="TEA CrytalNest" />

==History==
===A secret world===
[[File:Kashyyyk_TEA.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The visit of a Shashay scout to Kashyyyk (pictured) changed the galactic reputation of Crytal Nest's native inhabitants.]]
By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="22 BBY">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_4.html|Study: Pollution Standards Still Endanger Many}}, which mentions off-world [[Shashay]], is set on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] "13:3:28," which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> members of the [[Shashay]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_4.html|Study: Pollution Standards Still Endanger Many}}</ref> that was [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Crytal Nest<ref name="Abduction" /> had already traveled offworld.<ref name="HNN" /> For many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] during the existence of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its successor, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Shashay [[Starship/Legends|starships]] [[Scout/Legends|explored]] nearby [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] and carried out low-profile [[Merchant/Legends|trading]] with small settlements. Approximately a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Shashay became more prominent in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] after a Shashay [[Unidentified Shashay scout pilot|scout]] was forced to land her damaged [[Scout ship/Legends|ship]] on<ref name="Abduction" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet<ref name="TEA ">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and set into motion events that led to the Shashay becoming highly sought-after as [[Musician/Legends|singers]] throughout the Empire.<ref name="Abduction" />

Eventually, the Shashay became more reclusive.<ref name="Abduction" /> Rightfully fearing that the Empire would exploit Crytal Nest if it was discovered,<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> the Shashay kept the planet's location a closely guarded secret. Very few Shashay were allowed to travel offworld, and Crytal Nest's coordinates<ref name="Abduction" /> were never written down<ref name="AE" /> or recorded into [[Memory bank/Legends|memory banks]] of [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computers]]; instead, Shashay [[Navigator/Legends|navigators]] manually entered them from memory.<ref name="Abduction" /> The Shashay had such strong communal ties to their homeworld that every member of the species would [[Death/Legends|die]] before<ref name="AE" /> divulging Crytal Nest's location. For many years, that attitude protected the planet from being discovered by the [[Imperial Survey Corps/Legends|Imperial Survey Corps]].<ref name="Abduction" />

A few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before the Battle of Yavin, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] decided to seek the aid of the Shashay in its [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|struggle]] against the Empire. The Shashay initially politely resisted the Alliance's advances, but the natives of Crytal Nest became more receptive as the atrocities committed by the Empire continued to grow in scope. Around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Battle of Yavin, the Alliance and the [[Nestmothers of the Shashay]] had nearly concluded negotiations for a treaty of mutual support. The terms of the treaty provided for the establishment of a Rebel base on Crytal Nest—a facility that the Alliance believed would be completely secure due to the planet's secret location. However, the Nestmothers still wished to be convinced of the Alliance's good faith, resourcefulness, and ability to defeat the Empire.<ref name="Abduction" />

===The abduction of Crying Dawn Singer===
{{Quote|Here are the Rebellion's demands: first, a payment of 10 billion Imperial Credits, followed by a donation of equipment valued at that same amount. Second, we want you to support a Rebel Alliance base on your precious homeworld.|Pre-recorded holotransmission of Yearo Seville to the Shashay|<ref name="Abduction" />}}
[[Chief Administrator/Legends|Chief Administrator]] [[Braig Farool]] of [[TransGalMeg Industries, Incorporated]] and his brother [[Nak Farool|Nak]], [[Special Assistant]] to Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Nile Owen]] of<ref name="Abduction" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="OuterRimSectors">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]], devised around that time a [[Operation 45RA|plan]] to simultaneously and publicly discredit the Rebel Alliance and Owen. To that end, they had [[Crying Dawn Singer]], a famous Shashay performer, kidnapped while he was performing in the Rayter [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. When [[Zeke Rondel]], the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the Alliance [[Berrol's Donn Rebel base|base]] on the celestial body [[Berrol's Donn/Legends|Berrol's Donn]], tasked a team with finding and retrieving the singer, he informed them of the importance of the mission to the ongoing negotiations over establishing a Rebel facility on Crytal Nest.<ref name="Abduction" />

[[File:Crying_Dawn_Singer1.png|thumb|left|300px|The affiliation of Crytal Nest depended on the fate of Crying Dawn Singer (pictured).]]
Around that time, Nak Farool arranged for the production and sequential public transmission of a group of [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] recordings that implicated the Alliance in the abduction of Crying Dawn Singer. In one of the recordings, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Yearo Seville]] claimed to be an Alliance leader holding the performer hostage and demanded that, in return for Crying Dawn Singer's release, the Shashay support the establishment of a Rebel base on Crytal Nest. Farool also sent Owen a [[Entry 527|message]] reporting on the operation's progress involving the kidnapping—with the Moff being led to believe that its ultimate goal was the discreditation of the Alliance and the alignment of the Shashay with the Empire. In the message, Farool expressed his confidence that the Imperials' actions would shortly result in the Crytal Nest system coming under the Empire's control.<ref name="Abduction" />

Unbeknown to Owen, the recordings ordered by Farool also included a [[Media/Legends|news]] report on a supposed rescue of Crying Dawn Singer by an elite force organized by TransGalMeg Industries, Incorporated. In that recording, Braig Farool claimed that he and the "liberated" Shashay performer were preparing to meet with the leaders of Crytal Nest in order to negotiate a treaty that would bring the Shashay under Imperial protection. That holotransmission, however, was never sent due to Rondel's Rebel team capturing the Imperial [[II-xC Maintenance/Broadcast Ship]] responsible for the transmissions.<ref name="Abduction" />

===Alliance prevails===
{{Quote|You will rescue me and Crying Dawn Singer from this planet, destroy Moff Owen and the ''Impending Doom'' and escort us to the Shashay homeworld, where we will negotiate the treaty which makes the Shashay the servants of the Emperor and myself the ruler of the Ettarue arm of the galaxy.|Braig Farool, to a team of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Abduction" />}}
Later, the Rebel agents confronted Braig, who was holding Crying Dawn Singer captive, on the planet [[Narg]]. By that time Owen, who had learned of the Farools' plot, had executed Nak and arrived in Narg's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in his personal [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]], the ''[[Impending Doom]]'', to rescue the Shashay singer and arrest and kill Braig as well. Being aware of those facts, Farool had become delusional and, armed with a [[A280 blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], demanded that the Rebels "destroy" Owen and the ''Impending Doom'' and take him and Crying Dawn Singer to Crytal Nest. There, Farool expected to negotiate a treaty that would render the Shashay a part of the Rayter sector and the Empire—an accomplishment for which he further believed that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] would richly reward him.<ref name="Abduction" />

{{RPG|''[[The Abduction]]''}}
The Rebel team eventually rescued Crying Dawn Singer and returned to the Alliance. The agents were subsequently among the delegates chosen to travel to Crytal Nest, where they observed ceremonies and the ratification of the treaty between the Alliance and the Shashay.<ref name="Abduction" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Shashay}}
[[File:Shashay-AE.png|thumb|right|300px|Crytal Nest was the homeworld of the Shashay.]]
Crytal Nest was the homeworld of the sentient Shashay species, which had evolved from [[Bird/Legends|birds]]. At some point, the Shashay acquired the ability of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel, and their avian [[Brain/Legends|brains]] easily adapted the skills necessary for three-dimensional terrestrial flight to deal with the intricacies of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], allowing for members of the species to become excellent [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogators]].<ref name="Abduction" />

The Shashay spoke a [[Shashay language|language]] other than [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], organized their society around [[Nestclan]]s, and were governed by the Nestmothers of the Shashay. Although life on Crytal Nest was regimented, most Shashay were content to remain on their homeworld, with the exception of those who sought adventure and excitement and accordingly became offworld traders. The highly individualistic Shashay Crying Dawn Singer also did not enjoy living on Crytal Nest, and since he was a talented singer even among the Shashay, the Nestmothers allowed him to leave the planet in order to [[Music/Legends|perform]] in the wider [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]—also reasoning that the profit Crying Dawn Singer would earn through his performances would be beneficial to his entire species.<ref name="Abduction" />

==Behind the scenes==
Crytal Nest was introduced in ''[[The Abduction]]'', a [[1992]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure authored by [[Chuck Truett]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Abduction" /> The [[1998]] West End Games sourcebook ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' consistently misspells the planet's name as "Crystal Nest."<ref name="AE" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Crytal Nest system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square H-20.<ref name="TEA CrytalNest" />

In ''The Abduction'', if the player characters fail to rescue Crying Dawn Singer, Moff Nile Owen attempts to force the Shashay to reveal the location of Crytal Nest. In the process, Crying Dawn Singer is killed and his species breaks off all relations with the Rebel Alliance, essentially dooming its operations in the Rayter sector. This article assumes the events of the adventure play out as described.<ref name="Abduction" />
